problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch must handle minor Claude JSON key drift and fail closed with explicit blockers on invalid agent JSON.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Minor recoverable JSON drift is recorded, unrecoverable JSON receives EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and PRD readiness stays blocked.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Minor recoverable JSON drift is recorded, unrecoverable JSON receives EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and PRD readiness stays blocked.
risks: Malformed provider output could be silently accepted or produce unclear blockers that hide readiness failure.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch already handles minor Claude JSON key drift with repair logic and fails closed with explicit blockers (EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON) on unrecoverable JSON, keeping PRD readiness blocked.

evidence.summary: parseJsonCandidate function repairs minor JSON drift including: line ranges (42 → 42-88), backslash escapes, extra quotes in object keys, and trailing commas. Returns { parsed, repaired, error } structure.
evidence.why: This is the core repair logic that handles Claude's minor JSON key/value drift before failing closed on unrecoverable errors.

evidence.summary: extractJsonObject function applies multiple extraction strategies: direct parse, fenced code blocks (```json), and fallback slicing from first '{' to last '}'. Returns { parsed, repaired, error } structure.
evidence.why: This ensures repair logic is applied across common Claude output formats before declaring JSON unrecoverable.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult returns explicit EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON error code when parsed is null, includes parseError in missing array, and returns blocked verdict with structured error information.
evidence.why: This is the fail-closed behavior that ensures invalid agent JSON produces explicit blockers instead of being silently accepted.

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues function checks for error_code in agent results and creates a blocker with that exact code, ensuring EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON becomes a readiness blocker.
evidence.why: This is the PRD readiness integration that ensures invalid JSON errors keep the demand blocked.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness iterates through required evidence agent roles (explorer, verifier for single_agent policy), calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues for each, and aggregates all blockers. prd_ready is false when blockers exist.
evidence.why: This is the readiness gate that confirms invalid JSON blockers prevent PRD readiness from passing.

evidence.summary: test 'repairs minor Claude JSON key drift and records the repair' verifies that parseJsonCandidate repairs line ranges, escaped backslashes, and extra quotes, and that provider_runs records json_repaired: true.
evidence.why: This confirms the repair logic works for the specific drift patterns mentioned in the demand problem.

evidence.summary: test 'invalid agent JSON fails closed with explicit blocker code' verifies that 'not json' output results in status: blocked, error_code: EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON in every agent result, and readiness blockers include the error code.
evidence.why: This confirms the fail-closed behavior and PRD readiness blocking work as specified in the demand acceptance criteria.

risks: If the repair logic does not cover new Claude drift patterns, valid JSON could be incorrectly rejected or invalid JSON could be incorrectly accepted.
If error_code is not properly propagated from normalizeAgentResult through to readiness inspection, blockers could be silently dropped.

result.notes: Evidence-dispatch already implements the specified behavior: minor recoverable JSON drift is repaired and recorded (json_repaired: true), unrecoverable JSON receives EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and PRD readiness stays blocked due to error_code blocker. No code changes required based on current implementation and test coverage.

agent_results.claim: Explorer evidence is verified: parseJsonCandidate (lines 330-346) repairs line ranges, backslash escapes, extra quotes, and trailing commas; extractJsonObject (lines 348-368) applies direct parse, fenced code block, and slicing strategies; normalizeAgentResult (lines 370-421) returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON when parsed is null and includes parseError in missing; evidenceResultBlockingIssues (lines 489-494) creates blocker with error_code; inspectDemandPrdReadiness (lines 710-727) iterates required roles and calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues; tests at lines 535-571 and 573-603 verify repair and fail-closed behavior.

evidence.summary: parseJsonCandidate function verified: repairs line range format (42-88), escaped backslashes, extra quotes in keys, and trailing commas. Returns { parsed, repaired, error } structure.
evidence.why: Confirms explorer claim about repair logic implementation at exact path and line numbers.

evidence.summary: extractJsonObject function verified: tries direct parse, fenced code block matching, and slicing from first { to last }. Returns { parsed, repaired, error } structure.
evidence.why: Confirms explorer claim about multi-strategy extraction implementation at exact path and line numbers.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult function verified: sets errorCode to EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON when parsed is null, includes parseError in missing array, returns blocked verdict with error_code in result.
evidence.why: Confirms explorer claim about fail-closed behavior with explicit error code at exact path and line numbers.

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues function verified: extracts error_code from result and pushes blocker with code: errorCode when present.
evidence.why: Confirms explorer claim about error_code propagation to blocker creation at exact path and line numbers.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness function verified: iterates requiredEvidenceRoles, calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues for each result, aggregates blockers. prd_ready stays false when blockers exist.
evidence.why: Confirms explorer claim about readiness gate implementation at exact path and line numbers.

evidence.summary: Test verified: repairs minor Claude JSON key drift (line ranges, backslash escapes, extra quotes), asserts result.status is pass and provider_runs includes json_repaired: true.
evidence.why: Confirms explorer claim about repair test coverage at exact path and line numbers.

evidence.summary: Test verified: invalid agent JSON (not json) results in status: blocked, all agent_results have error_code: EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and readiness blockers include that code.
evidence.why: Confirms explorer claim about fail-closed test coverage at exact path and line numbers.

risks: Explorer evidence paths and line numbers are accurate but code could change after verification.
Test coverage may not exhaust all possible Claude JSON drift patterns beyond the three specific cases tested.
Error propagation path from normalizeAgentResult through evidenceResultBlockingIssues to inspectDemandPrdReadiness assumes all intermediate functions pass error_code correctly.

result.notes: All explorer evidence verified against actual file content at specified paths and line numbers. No assumptions presented as facts. All evidence records include required scope field. No missing data identified. Claim that existing implementation handles minor JSON drift and fails closed is supported by verified code and tests.
